data.title: Asia stocks advance tracking global peers higher; investors assess China inflation data
data.description:
The Asia-Pacific equity markets saw a moderate rebound on Friday, drawing positive momentum from a strong performance on Wall Street as better-than-expected US jobless claims data alleviated fears of a recession. Investors digested data showing consumer prices in China rose more than expected in July, while producer prices remained deflationary.

Japan (NKY:IND) +0.56%. The Nikkei 225 Index rose on Friday, reversing losses from the previous session.

The Japanese yen depreciated past 147 per dollar, hovering near a one-week low, as the dollar strengthened on better-than-expected US jobless claims data.

China (SHCOMP) -0.27%. The Shanghai Composite fell on Friday, hovering close to six-month lows, as investors reacted to the latest inflation figures in China. Data showed that consumer prices in China rose more than expected in July, while producer prices remained deflationary.

China's vehicle sales shrank by 5.2% from a year earlier in July 2024, steeper than a 2.7% fall in the previous month.

Hong Kong (HSI) +1.38%. Shares in Hong Kong soared in early trade on Friday, rising for the third day as investors reacted to China's fresh data.

India (SENSEX) +0.95%. The stock market in India rose in early deals on Friday, rebounding from the previous session, supported by gains in all sectors, led by auto.

Australia (AS51) +1.25%. The S&P/ASX 200 Index jumped on Friday, recouping some losses from earlier this week.

In the U.S., on Thursday, all three major indexes ended higher, posted their biggest daily gain since November 2022, aided by a drop in unemployment claims that alleviated concerns about a potential economic slowdown.

U.S. stock futures held steady on Friday after rebounding sharply in the previous session: Dow +0.11%; S&P 500 +0.09%; Nasdaq +0.12%.

Bitcoin rallied more than 10% to above $60,000, hitting a one-week-high.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

More on Asia:

  • China's July consumer prices rise more than expected, producer prices remains deflationary

  • China's trade surplus below estimates as exports disappoint, imports grow more than estimated

  • Japan stocks rebound sharply, up more than 10% after global market selloff

  • Reserve Bank of Australia keeps cash rate unchanged at 4.35% for the sixth time
  • China's services PMI expands more than expected in July amid robust demand conditions

data.title: Skanska signs supplemental award worth $203M for data center in Georgia
data.description:
• Skanska (SKBSY) has received a supplemental award with an existing client to build a data center in Georgia, USA, it said on Friday.
  • The supplemental award is worth $203M, about SEK 2.1 billion, which will be included in the US order bookings for the third quarter of 2024.
  • The project includes the construction of a 244,090 square-foot data center, associated site work, and underground utilities. The scope also includes fitout of an administration space and five data halls.
  • Work is underway and is scheduled for completion in the first quarter of 2026.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Okeanis Eco Tankers Non-GAAP EPS of $1.23 beats by $0.33, revenue of $112M beats by $33.62M
data.description:
• Okeanis Eco Tankers press release (NYSE:ECO): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.23 beats by $0.33.
  • Revenue of $112M (-6.5% Y/Y) beats by $33.62M.
  • Time charter equivalent revenue of $79.4 million in Q2 2024, compared to $91.2 million in Q2 2023.
  • EBITDA and Adjusted EBITDA of $63.6 million and $63.9 million, respectively, in Q2 2024.
  • Fleetwide daily TCE rate of $64,900 per operating day in Q2 2024.
  • In Q3 2024 to date, 58% of the available VLCC spot days have been booked at an average TCE rate of $46,100 per day and 53% of the available Suezmax spot days have been booked at an average TCE rate of $58,000 per day.

data.title: ASE Technology reports higher July revenue
data.description:
• ASE Technology Holding (NYSE:ASX) Friday reported July revenues of NT$51,596 million, up 6.7% year-on-year, and a 10% jump sequentially.

  • Net revenues for ATM assembly, testing and material business rose 2.4% year-on-year to NT$ 27,443 million.

  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Bright Mountain Media amends credit agreement with Centre Lane Partners
data.description:
• Bright Mountain Media (OTCQB:BMTM) has amended its existing credit agreement with Centre Lane Partners, deferring certain interest and amortization payments for fiscal year 2024, it said in a filing.
  • Bright Mountain (OTCQB:BMTM) and its subsidiaries are parties to an Amended and Restated Senior Secured Credit Agreement between themselves, the lenders party thereto, and Centre Lane Partners Master Credit Fund II, L.P. as administrative agent and collateral agent, dated June 5, 2020.
  • Effective June 30th, 2024, the Credit Agreement was amended for a twentieth time.
  • The principal changes include: (i) Extending the maturity date of the Nineteenth Amendment Term Loan from June 30, 2024, to December 31, 2024; (ii) Changing the repayment of the Nineteenth Amendment Term Loan so that commencing September 30, the company commences repayment by making four monthly payments of principal and interest with the balance payable on December 31, 2024.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Dianthus Therapeutics GAAP EPS of -$0.51 beats by $0.04
data.description:
• Dianthus Therapeutics press release (NASDAQ:DNTH): Q2 GAAP EPS of -$0.51 beats by $0.04.
  • Cash Position - $360.7 million of cash, cash equivalents and short-term investments as of June 30, 2024 is projected to provide runway into the second half of 2027.

data.title: Tarsus Pharmaceuticals GAAP EPS of -$0.88 beats by $0.08, revenue of $40.81M beats by $8.76M
data.description:
• Tarsus Pharmaceuticals press release (NASDAQ:TARS): Q2 GAAP EPS of -$0.88 beats by $0.08.
  • Revenue of $40.81M beats by $8.76M.
  • As of June 30, 2024, cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities were $323.6 million, which includes the receipt of approximately $39.6 million of net proceeds from an initial draw on our new credit facility in April 2024.

data.title: 4D Molecular Therapeutics GAAP EPS of -$0.63 beats by $0.06, revenue of $0.01M misses by $0.68M
data.description:
• 4D Molecular Therapeutics press release (NASDAQ:FDMT): Q2 GAAP EPS of -$0.63 beats by $0.06.
  • Revenue of $0.005M (vs. $0.24M a year ago quarter) misses by $0.68M.
  • Cash and cash equivalents and marketable securities were $578 million as of June 30, 2024, as compared to $299 million as of December 31, 2023.
  • "We currently expect cash and cash equivalents to be sufficient to fund operations into the first half of 2027."

data.title: TELUS prices $700M debt offering via issuance of senior notes due 2031
data.description:
• TELUS (NYSE:TU) on Thursday, said that it has priced $700 million of senior unsecured notes with 7-year maturity.
  • The 4.65% notes, Series CAQ, were priced at $99.911 per $100 principal amount for an effective yield of 4.665% per annum until maturity, and will mature on August 13, 2031.
  • The offering's net proceeds will be utilized to repay outstanding debts, including commercial paper, and the reduction of cash outstanding under an arm's length securitization trust, allowing an affiliate of TELUS to borrow against trade and unbilled customer finance receivables, and for other general corporate purposes.
  • The notes are offered through a syndicate of agents led by BMO Capital Markets, CIBC Capital Markets and RBC Capital Markets.
  • The closing of the offering is expected to occur on or about August 13, 2024.

data.title: Zevra Therapeutics stock slides after pricing stock offering to raise $60 million
data.description:
• Zevra Therapeutics (NASDAQ:ZVRA) has announced the pricing of an underwritten public offering of 9.23 million shares of its common stock at $6.50 per share, for total gross proceeds of approximately $60.0 million.
  • All the common stock is being offered by Zevra.
  • The offering is expected to close on August 12, 2024.
  • In addition, Zevra has granted the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 1.38 million shares of its common stock at the public offering price.
  • ZVRA stock fell 11.1% to $6.57 after-hours on Thursday.
